body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

`multi-turn-conversation` chains three consecutive user prompts in one session, validating that the parser resets state between turns (`ready -> working -> ready` per turn) and accumulates per-turn Contribution. It shares the `basic-turn` primitive (transcript turn-end detection, requires `headless_mode`) but exercises it three times in one live session, so the load-bearing question is whether codex sustains a multi-turn REPL and whether the daemon flips back to `ready` between turns rather than only seeing a single-shot exit.

**agent_supports: yes.** Unlike the single-shot `codex exec` path used for `basic-turn`, codex ships an interactive REPL (`tui: true` in capabilities.json). The committed `drive-codex-interactive.sh` driver runs that REPL under tmux and walks send/wait_turn steps, blocking on each `event_msg/task_complete` line before sending the next prompt. Three send+wait_turn pairs are therefore expressible as one live session — the REPL persists across turns instead of exiting after the first reply.

**irrlicht_observes: yes.** The codex adapter watches rollout transcripts via `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser{...}}` (agent.go). The parser classifies each `task_complete` event_msg as `turn_done`, so `IsAgentDone()` fires once per turn and drives a `working -> ready` flip back at each turn boundary — exactly the state reset the spec asserts. Per-turn token accumulation is covered by `TestParser_MultiTurnTokenCount_UsesPerTurnSnapshot`, which proves the parser reads each turn's `token_count` snapshot rather than double-counting, yielding the rising cumulative `TotalTokens` across the three turns. `TestParser_TurnCompleteEmitsTurnDone` covers the turn-boundary classification. Because this is a live REPL session (not a headless single-shot exit), the daemon observes three full `working -> ready` arcs on the same rollout session_id, then `process_exited` only when the driver tears the REPL down.

No carve-outs apply. The matrix already records codex/basic-turn (the shared `basic-turn` coverage row) as yes/yes; this assessment ratifies that the same verdict holds when the turn is repeated three times in one interactive session.
caveats: Exact assistant wording and per-turn token counts vary run-to-run (model-output variance); the spec asserts only the structural three-turn ready→working→ready arc, not specific text or token totals.

sources.note: FilesUnderRoot + JSONLineParser transport; ExactName process match
task_complete -> turn_done so IsAgentDone fires once per turn, driving working->ready between turns
interactive REPL driver supports send/wait_turn/sleep/interrupt — sustains a multi-turn session under tmux
headless_mode: true, tui: true, structured_output_jsonl: true, cost_tracking: true
